sanskrit:
यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ।
समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते।।
transliteration:
yaṁ hi na vyathayanty ete puruṣaṁ puruṣarṣabha |
sama-duḥkha-sukhaṁ dhīraṁ so ’mṛtatvāya kalpate ||
literal_meaning: One who is not shaken by these (pairs of opposites), O bull among men, who remains balanced in pain and pleasure, steady—such a person is fit for immortality.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that emotional equanimity—not the absence of pain or joy, but the capacity to hold both without being hijacked—is the psychological prerequisite for liberation from cyclic suffering.
psychological_state: Heightened amygdala arousal; cognitive dissonance between warrior identity and attachment; searching for a regulatory model that does not require suppressing feelings or acting them out.
trigger_scenario: A mid-level manager asked to lay off beloved team members during a recession—heart races, palms sweat, mind cycles between quitting the job or obeying the order—seeking an inner stance that is neither cold nor overwhelmed.
anchor_text: Stay steady through pain and pleasure to transcend life's cycles.
